title: Write decimals and fractions shown on number lines
description: Given a point between 0 and 1 on a number line, write the value in fraction and decimal form using tenths and hundredths.
topic_path: Write decimals and fractions shown on number lines

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Identify the point on the number line and write it as both a fraction and a decimal.**

**Fraction:**     [[☃ numeric-input 1]]  

**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The number line between $0$ and $1$ is split into $10$ equal pieces.  

Each piece has a length of $\dfrac{1}{10}$ .  

[[☃ image 4]]

Starting at $0$, we move $\blueE{{7} \text{ lengths of } \dfrac{1}{10}}$ to get to the point at $\blueE{\dfrac{7}{10}}$ .

[[☃ image 2]]
$\blueE{\dfrac{7}{10}}$ can also be expressed as $\blueE{7 \text{ tenths}}$.

Let's use a place value chart to write $\blueE{7 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | ${7}$ |

We can write the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{7}{10}$

* Decimal: $0.7$

options.alt: A number line that has moving left to right, a starting mark at 0, nine unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at 1. There is a dot at the seventh tick mark to the right of 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Identify the point on the number line and write it as both a fraction and a decimal.**

**Fraction:**     [[☃ numeric-input 1]]  

**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The length between the labeled tick marks is $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$, or $\tealE{1 \text{ tenth}}$.

Let's zoom in.

There are $10$ equal distances between each $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\redE{\dfrac{1}{100}}$, or $\redE{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

The point is $\redE{ \text{7 hundredths}}$ after $\purpleD{0}$.

[[☃ image 2]]
$\blueE{\dfrac{7}{100}}$ can also be expressed as $\blueE{7 \text{ hundredths}}$.

Let's use a place value chart to write $\blueE{7 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal. 


Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | ${0}$ | ${7}$

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{7}{100}$

* Decimal: $0.07$

options.alt: A number line that has moving left to right, a starting mark at 0, nine unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled one-tenth, nine unlabeled tick marks, a tick mark labeled two-tenths, nine unlabeled tick marks, and an ending tick mark at three-tenths. There is a dot at the seventh tick mark to the right of 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Identify the point on the number line and write it as both a fraction and a decimal.**

**Fraction:**     [[☃ numeric-input 3]]  

**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints:
The length between the labeled tick marks is $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$, or $\tealE{1 \text{ tenth}}$.

Let's zoom in.

There are $10$ equal distances between each $\tealE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ on the number line. Those distances are each $\redE{\dfrac{1}{100}}$, or $\redE{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The point is $\redE{ \text{7 hundredths}}$ after $\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{10}}$.

[[☃ image 2]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{10}}$ and $\redE{7\text{ hundredths}}$ is $\blueE{0.17}$.

Tens |Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|${0}$|${.}$  | $\purpleD{1}$ | $\redE{7}$

We can read the number as $\blueE{17\text{ hundredths}}$.

We can write the fraction as $\blueE{\dfrac{17}{100}}$.

We can express the location of the point on the number line in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{17}{100}$

* Decimal: $0.17$

There is a dot at the seventh tick mark to the right of one-tenth.
